original_hypothesis.title: CrossChainOwned onMessageInvocation Self-Call Without Reentrancy Protection
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: CrossChainOwned.onMessageInvocation deliberately lacks nonReentrant and executes address(this).call(txdata) before incrementing nextTxId (nextTxId++ is post-increment in emit statement). During the self-call execution, nextTxId has not yet been incremented. If the self-call somehow triggers another bridge message processing, the same txId could theoretically be used, or state mutations during the call could affect the post-call state in unexpected ways.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The code comment explicitly says nonReentrant is omitted because 'this function will re-enter the contract'. The self-call with arbitrary txdata combined with no reentrancy guard creates a potential for unexpected state interactions if the called function has side effects that affect onMessageInvocation's postconditions.

proof_of_signal.pattern: address(this).call(txdata) before nextTxId++; explicitly no nonReentrant guard

preconditions: Bridge contract is compromised or has a reentrancy path
txdata triggers a function that can re-enter onMessageInvocation via bridge

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: nextTxId not incremented during self-call execution; potential for re-use
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No reentrancy guard; no pre-increment of nextTxId

analysis_questions: Can address(this).call(txdata) trigger a path that calls back into onMessageInvocation?
What functions can the self-call access given msg.sender == address(this)?
Does the bridge allow nested message processing?
validation_requirements: Map all callable functions via self-call and their side effects
Test if bridge allows nested onMessageInvocation calls
Verify nextTxId state during self-call execution

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: If the bridge allows nested message processing, a compromised L1 owner could craft txdata that triggers another bridge call during execution. Since nextTxId hasn't been incremented, the nested call could theoretically pass the txId check again, executing multiple cross-chain transactions with a single txId.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Multiple admin operations executed with single cross-chain txId
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All L2 contract users if admin operations are malicious
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Depends on the specific admin operations executed

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No reentrancy test for onMessageInvocation self-call pattern

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
